what_it_does: A reply letter from the Visual Factory session to the Design DNA Pack session that maps proposed design-DNA layers onto Visual Factory ingestion shapes, gives concrete token/component/template examples, answers decisions B-K, and asks for a marked-up template plus a small dist slice.
observations: Frames the two systems as compatible layer mappings: primitives, registries, templates, dials, voice, context, invariants, and provenance.
Defines an ingestion contract around dot.case tokens, DTCG token records, component-definition JSON, cascade rules, design-system variants, and predicate invariants.
Includes JSONC examples for an organism brand icon and a current-to-solution template with zones, slots, styling token references, dials, and provenance.
Specifies serialization as per-layer JSON files plus a dna.manifest.json in a dist-style bundle.
Chooses both anchor/precompiled stops and OKLCH interpolation for dials, with warmth treated as continuous and other dimensions as discrete candidates.
Closes with two requested deliverables: one template record in the shown template shape and a tiny dist slice containing tokens, one template, invariants, and a two-stop warmth example.
standouts: The file translates abstract design-DNA layers into concrete registry, cascade, schema, and lint ingestion shapes.
It supplies executable-looking JSONC examples rather than only prose agreement.
It treats provenance, voice, routing, and invariants as importable data alongside visual tokens and templates.
conventions: Uses a letter-reply format with sections A and F expanded, then decisions B-K as bullets.
Uses Markdown headings, tables, checkmarks, inline code paths, and JSONC snippets.
Describes roles through slots, accepted address kinds, token-referenced styling, and optional promotion into component definitions.
Keeps source token names in dot.case while describing rendered CSS variables as kebab-case custom properties.
Represents provenance in context.$provenance or DTCG-style extension fields.
concerns: The file references external repositories, branches, schemas, database tables, and tests that are not available within the file itself.
Several schema examples are JSONC and illustrative, so they are not strict machine-ingestible JSON as written.
Some status claims are asserted from the sender's side without evidence inside this file.
notes: Brief someone on this as the Visual Factory side's proposed ingestion contract and compatibility map for a Design DNA Pack.
questions: What exact field names should be locked after receiving the requested marked-up template record?
What predicate expression language or runtime is expected for the invariant objects?
What exact manifest schema is expected for dna.manifest.json?
How should DTCG aliases, resolved convenience values, and cascade scopes be represented together in the tiny dist slice?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it replies with a concrete target contract, sample grammar, decision answers, current status, and requested next inputs.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make a separate Design DNA Pack deliverable importable by the Visual Factory by translating conceptual layers into specific file, schema, registry, cascade, and lint shapes.
novelty: The file combines design-token packaging, component schema examples, dial interpolation strategy, AI voice/context routing, and invariant predicates into one negotiated handoff contract.
contribution: It contributes a file-local contract for representing a design substrate ledger as DTCG tokens, component/template definitions, cascade-aware dial variants, role presets, invariant predicates, voice/context routing data, and provenance fields that a fused system could preserve as interoperable ingestion shapes.
summary_for_embedding: This is a Visual Factory reply that maps Design DNA Pack layers to concrete DTCG token, component registry, template, dial, invariant, voice, routing, versioning, and provenance structures with JSONC examples and requested next deliverables.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to align two design-system sessions around a shared importable contract and to request a marked-up template plus a minimal dist bundle for end-to-end validation.
